steps.title: Identify the structure on each side
steps.content: Left: "...a review board of experts...gathers to" — an independent clause with the verb gathers and the infinitive to beginning an infinitive phrase. Right: "which sites to add next to the growing registry" — the direct object (embedded question) of select. The blank supplies the verb select.

steps.title: Apply the rule: no punctuation between a verb and its direct object
steps.content:
The verb select must connect directly to its object "which sites..." without any punctuation.
  • "select,": "gathers to select, which sites..." — a comma cannot separate a verb from its direct object; this is a disruptive punctuation error. ✗
  • "select:": "gathers to select: which sites..." — a colon requires an independent clause on the left; "gathers to select" is not one. ✗
  • "select;": "gathers to select; which sites..." — a semicolon joins two independent clauses, but "which sites to add..." is a dependent clause, not an IC. ✗
  • "select": "gathers to select which sites to add next to the growing registry" — verb and object connect cleanly. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
